raw_data.notes: Secondary mediation analysis. No direct treatment effect estimate (OR/RR/HR/MD/SMD) reported for substance use composite. Table 1 reports descriptive stats by group: Average drinks/week at 6-month: BA=13.94(31.73), BMI=11.10(24.20), BMI+B=10.15(26.99); at 12-month: BA=14.83(28.23), BMI=12.76(31.01), BMI+B=11.60(26.87). At-risk drinking at 6-month: BA 24.2%, BMI 23.7%, BMI+B 18.8%. Path coefficients from mediation model (Table 2) for BMI+B on alcohol use at 6mo: B=0.22, SE=0.15 (ns). Three-arm trial (BA n=200, BMI n=203, BMI+B n=193) with no pairwise effect size reported.

source_quote: BMI + B compared with BA and BMI showed significantly greater post-intervention reductions in alcohol use across the 3-, 6-, and 12-month follow-ups among all patients (Field et al., 2014)

raw_data.notes: Self-reported days of alcohol or illicit drug use during first month of treatment. MET-PS (exp) vs TAU (ctrl). No significant treatment effect (Z=1.19, p>.05). For full 4-month period: TAU mean=4.70(SD=8.87, n=89), MET mean=4.26(SD=8.73, n=84), also ns (Z=1.11, p>.05). Urine toxicology positive rate first month: TAU=27.8%, MET=25.4% (ns).

source_quote: Analysis of the days of drug or alcohol use during the first month of treatment found no significant Treatment (Z=1.19, p>.05), Treatment x Week (Z=0.56, p>.05), or Site x Treatment x Week (X2=2.15, df=2, p>.05) effects but did reveal a significant Week effect (Z=-2.40, p<.05).

raw_data.notes: Percent Days Abstinent (PDA) is the primary substance use outcome. Baseline PDA: BMI+SC=25.8(35.5, n=53), SC=23.6(29.4, n=52). Overall follow-up PDA: M=51.1, SD=39.8 (n=73, groups combined). No group-specific follow-up means reported. Regression Table 3: BMI+SC main effect on follow-up PDA: beta=-.10, t=-.88, R2=.01 (ns). The significant finding was a F1 x BMI+SC interaction (R2=.07, p<.05), not a main treatment effect. n at follow-up: 36 BMI+SC, 37 SC.

source_quote: Study condition was entered on Step One, and Psychopathy F1 on Step Two. There was no main effect for either of these variables. The grand-mean centered F1 X study condition interaction term, entered on Step Three, was statistically significant (change F = 5.45, change R2 = .07, p < .05).
